identity.canonical_title: Field Equations on a Trawinistic Manifold

theory.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic field equations are obtained by varying the Trawin action and setting the first variation to zero. They combine the Trawinistic Laplacian, the anchor potential, and curvature or torsion couplings into one dynamical law.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This is the variational origin of the field equation.

external_sources.title: Field Equations on a Trawinistic Manifold
external_sources.canonical_claim: The Trawinistic field equations are obtained by varying the Trawin action and setting the first variation to zero. They combine the Trawinistic Laplacian, the anchor potential, and curvature or torsion couplings into one dynamical law.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Action principles) supports the physical law (Euler–Lagrange equations) by showing that Variational principles state that varying the action and setting its first variation to zero yields the Euler–Lagrange equations—the field equations of the system.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: Variational principles state that varying the action and setting its first variation to zero yields the Euler–Lagrange equations—the field equations of the system. This supports deriving Trawinistic field equations by varying the Trawin action and setting the first variation to zero.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.
